data.description:
## What
`rebar review-code` assembles its diff with a TWO-dot range `git diff base..head`
(`src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py`). When `head` is behind `base` (reviewing a PR branch
not caught up to `origin/main`), a two-dot diff shows `head` relative to base's CURRENT tip, so
every commit merged into `base` since the branch point appears as a spurious deletion. This
inflates the review with a revert of unrelated work and produces phantom-deletion findings that
cite real line numbers but describe changes the PR never made. Reported upstream from a client
project: a 7-file PR reviewed as 11 files with ~720 phantom deletions; 3 of 5 findings were
artifacts.

## Approach
Switch both diff invocations in `assemble_diff_context` from `base..head` to the THREE-dot
`base...head` (merge-base) form, matching git's own PR-review convention. Three-dot diffs `head`
against the merge-base of base and head, excluding unrelated commits on `base` after the branch
point. The change is limited to the `diff_text is None` git path; the supplied-`diff_text` offline
seam is unaffected.

## Scope / files
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` — two `_git` diff calls (`diff` and `diff --name-only`).
- `tests/unit/test_code_review_assemble.py` — new behavioral regression test.

## Acceptance criteria
- A branch behind its base reviews ONLY the branch's own changed files; commits merged into base
  after the branch point are not surfaced as deletions.
- New regression test `test_diff_is_merge_base_three_dot_not_two_dot` builds a real repo with a
  branch behind main and asserts `changed_files == ["feat.py"]` (no phantom `other.py`); it fails
  under two-dot and passes under three-dot.
- lint, typecheck, and the assemble test module stay green.

## Out of scope
Run-to-run non-determinism and the supply-chain/severity findings from the same upstream report
are tracked separately.
data.title: code-review --base uses two-dot diff; reviewing a branch behind base surfaces phantom deletions
